description: Integration: the invoice template editor renders Design, Preview, and Transforms tabs together for editable templates.
Regression: switching between Design, Preview, and Transforms preserves existing designer workspace state.
Unit: designer workspace snapshot includes transform source binding, output binding, and ordered operations.
Unit: importing a template with no transforms produces empty/default transform workspace state.
Unit: importing a template with transforms preserves operation IDs, order, source binding, and output binding.
Unit: exporting workspace with no transforms omits the transforms block from generated AST.
Unit: exporting workspace with transforms generates a valid AST transforms block with the authored order and fields.
Regression: an existing non-transformed template can still be loaded, previewed, and saved unchanged.
Integration: source collection selector lists all collection bindings defined in the current template AST/workspace.
Integration: source collection selector updates the source data panel when a different collection binding is chosen.
Integration: source data panel shows binding ID, row count, and field discovery when sample preview data is available.
Integration: transform pipeline renders cards in authored order and highlights the selected transform for editing.
Integration: adding a transform inserts a new pipeline card and selects it for inspection.
Integration: deleting a selected transform removes it from the pipeline and keeps selection state stable.
Integration: duplicating a transform copies its configuration with a distinct operation identity.
Integration: reordering transforms updates pipeline order and generated AST order consistently.
Integration: filter inspector edits field, operator, and value and the generated AST reflects the change.
Integration: sort inspector supports one or more sort keys with direction metadata and preserves order.
Integration: group inspector edits group key field and optional strategy metadata.
Integration: aggregate inspector can add, edit, and remove aggregate definitions such as sum/count/avg.
Integration: output binding editor persists a non-empty binding ID into workspace and generated AST.
Integration: output preview shows transformed flat rows when the pipeline does not group.
Integration: output preview shows grouped rows with key and aggregates when the pipeline includes group + aggregate.
Integration: grouped output preview updates immediately when group key or aggregate definitions change.
Integration: a dynamic table can switch its source binding from a raw collection to the transform output binding.
Integration: dynamic-table preview renders transformed/grouped data correctly after source binding is changed.
Integration: table column mapping UI offers transformed row paths such as key and aggregates.sumTotal for grouped output.
Integration: selecting a different output binding shape refreshes available table column mapping options.
Validation: UI blocks or warns on illegal transform sequences that the evaluator does not support after grouped output.
Validation: transform-specific errors are surfaced inline with enough context to identify the offending operation.
Regression: valid transform pipelines do not show false-positive validation errors.
Integration: authoritative preview compiles successfully for a transformed template with filter/sort/group/aggregate operations.
Integration: authoritative preview rendered HTML includes grouped/aggregated invoice rows when the layout table targets the output binding.
Regression: read-only code tab includes the generated transforms block once the template has authored transforms.
Regression: read-only code tab remains valid generated AST JSON for templates with no transforms.
Round-trip: load a transformed template, make no changes, save, and confirm transforms remain identical after reopen.
Round-trip: reorder transforms, save, reopen, and confirm both workspace order and generated AST order are preserved.
Round-trip: update a group/aggregate pipeline, save, reopen, and confirm output preview still matches the saved transform configuration.
Regression: templates without transforms still pass the existing preview pipeline and design workspace tests.
Unit: AST import/export round-trip remains deterministic for transformed templates.
Unit: grouped output binding resolution exposes key/items/aggregates through the same collection resolution path used by the renderer.
Smoke: aggregation-first workflow can reproduce the example use case of grouping repeated invoice rows into rolled-up table rows.
